chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No.D-1593 of 2023 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For order on office objection. For hearing of MA No.1901/2024. For hearing of main case. 12.02.2026 Mr. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, A.A.G. Sindh along with Akhlaq Ahmed Kalwar Deputy Director (Recruitment) SPSC Hyderabad. None present for the petitioner. No intimation is furnished. The record reflects that on consecutive dates, i.e. 19.12.2023, 17.01.2024 and 14.02.2024, neither the petitioner nor his learned counsel appeared. The petition has been pending since the year 2023 without any meaningful progress. It is a settled principle that a litigant invoking the constitutional jurisdiction of this Court must pursue the matter with due diligence and seriousness. The repeated and unexplained absence of the petitioner clearly demonstrates lack of his interest to proceed with the instant matter. Even otherwise, on merits, the grievance of the petitioner does not warrant interference. The petitioner has challenged the qualification for the post of Subject Specialist Sindhi (BPS-17) However, as per the comments submitted by respondent No.2/Sindh Public Service Commission, the required qualification for the post of Subject Specialist Sindhi (BPS-17) in Recruitment Rules and Advertisement was “Master or equivalent Degree (Sixteen Years) education in relevant subject with minimum 50% marks or equivalent marks from H.E.C. recognized University but when the application of petitioner come under scrutiny it revealed that he did not possess required qualification i.e. having less than 50% marks in M.A. Sindhi and the Commission is bound to act strictly in accordance with the recruitment rules and the requisition furnished by the concerned Administrative Department.

The petitioner has challenged the qualification for the post of Subject Specialist Sindhi (BPS-17) However, as per the comments submitted by respondent No.2/Sindh Public Service Commission, the required qualification for the post of Subject Specialist Sindhi (BPS-17) in Recruitment Rules and Advertisement was “Master or equivalent Degree (Sixteen Years) education in relevant subject with minimum 50% marks or equivalent marks from H.E.C. recognized University but when the application of petitioner come under scrutiny it revealed that he did not possess required qualification i.e. having less than 50% marks in M.A. Sindhi and the Commission is bound to act strictly in accordance with the recruitment rules and the requisition furnished by the concerned Administrative Department. It is not vested with authority to alter, vary or deviate from the prescribed eligibility criteria or conditions embodied in the recruitment rules. Therefore, the Commission being a recommending authority is under obligation to follow the requirement for any post provided by administrative department where appointment is to be made. In these circumstances, on account of non-prosecution as well as on merits, no case for interference is made out. Consequently, the instant petition is dismissed along with the listed application. JUDGE JUDGE *Abdullah Channa/PS*
